q: SA's Bill of Rights is in the:
et: Bill of Rights: Chapter 2 of SA's 1996 Constitution.
NSFAS = National Student Financial Aid Scheme.

q: The most effective study technique is:
et: Active recall (testing yourself) is 2-3× more effective than passive re-reading.
Healthy relationships: mutual respect, trust, honesty, equality.
GBV has multiple causes: gender inequality, patriarchal norms, poverty, alcohol.
Exercise releases endorphins and improves both physical and mental health.

q: Section 10 of the SA Constitution guarantees:
et: Section 10: "Everyone has inherent dignity and the right to have their dignity respected."
RICE: Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation for minor sports injuries.
Peer pressure: influence from people in your peer group to conform to their behaviour.

q: The NSC minimum for bachelor's degree entry:
et: Bachelor's entry: 30% in Home Language + 50% in 4 other designated subjects.
